title: Impillagers
description: A total swamp overhaul mod with many new (and very quirky) residents to discover.
body:
![Mod Title: Impillagers Swamp Overhaul](https://github.com/Thomas-Nurse/Impillagers/raw/02682fc3fb284e3ac8097538397a8d92bfbc561d/images/mod_title_background.png)
##
***An extensive Vanilla+ overhaul of the swamps, bringing them to life with more foliage, new blocks, structures, creatures and many new secrets to discover!*** ![Swamp Banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6cd7d3bf224ed0667ea86dece980a7ba499f1074.png)

The Primary Features of the Mod Include:

  • A Complete Overhaul of the Swamp Biome
  • A New Complete Wood Set
  • An Expanded Block Set for Mud & Mud Bricks
  • A New Block Set for Dung & Dung Bricks
  • A Whole New Type of Trader Mobs called the Impillagers
  • A Set of 3 Unique Professions For the Impillagers
  • A New Type of Golem called a Dung Golem
  • An Expansive list of New Items
  • A New Set of Enchantments

Check the sections below for more details about some of the main features:

The Swamp
Purple Heart Trees
Swamps now have their own native type of tree called the Purple Heart. These trees are generally larger than a standard oak but smaller than a fancy oak. Purple Heart trees grow vines on their leaves and trunk. Purple Hearts feature a full Purple Colored Wood Set. (See in Block Sets)
Sinking Mud
Watch your step when in swamps, the mud near water may just be a new variant called Sinking Mud. Most entities that walk onto sinking mud will quickly find themselves sinking into its muddy embrace. If you're going to travel through the boggiest parts of the swamp, you should bring some Leather Boots or be prepared to meet the consequences.
Firefly Bushes
Firefly Bushes have been backported and expanded upon. These bushes can be found all over the swamps and during the night they will illuminate the sky with bright fireflies. Use a bottle on a Firefly bush and you can catch the Fireflies to be released wherever you want from the bottle!
Belladonna
The Belladonna is a new unique small flower, native to the swamp. This flower is poisonous and will poison any entity that comes into contact with it. Belladonnas can be crafted into purple dye.
Swamp Reeds
Swamp Reeds are a new type of aquatic foliage found in the shallow waters of swamps. They generate in dense groups and can be crafted into 2 Brown Dye.
Block Sets
Expanded Mud Set
The Mud block set has been greatly expanded with:
  • Packed Mud Stairs, Slabs & Walls
  • Mossy Bricks including Stairs, Slabs & Walls
  • Cracked Bricks
  • Chiseled Bricks
  • Pillars
Dung Set
A New Dung block set has been introduced with:
  • Dung Blocks
  • Packed Dung including Stairs, Slabs & Walls
  • Mossy Bricks including Stairs, Slabs & Walls
  • Cracked Bricks
  • Chiseled Bricks
  • Pillars
Purple Heart Wood Set
A purple colored wood called Purple Heart set has been introduced with:
  • Logs
  • Planks including Stairs & Slabs
  • Fences & Fence Gates
  • Signs & Hanging Signs
  • Doors & Trapdoors
  • Buttons & Pressure Plates
  • Normal & Chest Boats
Mobs
Impillagers
Impillagers are a new trader class of mob. They live in a unique village, only found in swamp biomes. (See Structures) Impillagers have their own unique set of professions that are independent of those of the villagers. They are neutral in nature, however they actively fight hostile mobs & attack Villagers.
Dung Golem
Dung Golems are a new type of golem. They are crafted from a Dung Block and a Carved Pumpkin. Impillagers naturally spawn these golems to help them in combat. Dung Golems shoot Dung out of their arm canon to attack. As a result of being made from dung, these golems are permanently Smelly.
Impillager Professions
Dung Collector

The Impillagers aren't exactly the cleanest but they are certainly crafty, this one has been collecting the all the dung around the village and turning it into blocks!

Dung Collectors use the Waste Basket as their job block, it automatically collects dung from nearby farm animals.
Banker

The Impillagers love to trade in gold, so much that they began to run out of gold to use! Banker Impillagers think it's a good idea to trade with outsiders to improve the flow of gold in the village. Just be careful though, like any Imp, Bankers are very crafty and some of the Gold Coins don't seem right recently...

Bankers use the Vault as their job block, a small lockable storage container! A simple crouch-right-click and nobody can get to your stuff.
Musician

Some Impillagers really do love their music, spending all day jamming out on the dance floor and listening to their favourite tunes. These Imps are always wanting to find the next best track to add to their collection and will happily part with their coin for it.

Musicians use the Jukebox as their job block.

Structures
Swamp Ruins
Swamps now have their own unique ruins for you to discover! Make sure to bring your Brush. Each Swamp Ruin has one Frog Mask, don't miss it as it will be your guide to the illusive and rare Frog Temple.
Frog Temple
Not Much is known about the history of the mighty Frog Temple, all we know is that current the residents certainly weren't the ones that built it. The Frog Temple has become the heart of the Impillager Village. Rumor is that the Impillagers didn't do a very good job of looting it, take a look around and you might find some interesting artifacts.
Single Village House

Impillagers find themselves vulnerable down on the ground, even in the relative safety of the swamp. As a result they built their houses in the trees surrounding the Frog Temple.

We don't know what drives this behavior, maybe it's something to do with their past before coming to the swamps?
Double Tree House
The Impillagers like to connect their tree houses together if they are close enough. After all, why go down the the ground when you can simply use a bridge?
Dung Collector's House
The Dung Collector has made this little spot their home, it may look like a bush but the Dung Collector thought it was the perfect place for the their collection. It might smell a bit though.
Musician's House
This used to be some kind of statue but the Musician decided that there is nothing better than a community dance floor. You'll often find the Musician by the jukebox, picking out the next best track!
Explosive Experts's House
You should watch your step in this one, this small ruin was taken over by the self-proclaimed Explosives Expert to store their stuff. It won't explode. Probably.
##

Impillagers generally should be compatible with any mod that doesn't override or remove the Vanilla Swamp

NeoForge: Impillagers is built as a Fabric mod. However, we Officially Support NeoForge via the fantastic Sinytra Connector Project! All versions as of 1.5.0 on 1.21.1 are built with NeoForge in mind and we aim for full compatibility with it.
List of known mod compatibility with Impillagers
Mod
Compatible?
Compatible Since
Reason
Terralith
Yes
>=1.6.0
Added Datapack patch to override Terralith's swamps
William Wythers Overhauled Overworld
Yes
>=1.6.0
Added Datapack patch to override WWOO's swamps
Cliff Under a Tree
Yes
>=1.6.0
Added Datapack patch to override CliffTree's swamps
World Preview
Yes
>=1.6.0
Added Datapack patch to add Impillagers Structures to the map
Vanilla Backport
Yes
>=1.6.0
Fixed bug that caused a crash on launch

We want to support as many mods as possible and work hard to bring compatibility patches. If you discover an incompatibility, please feel free to report it to our Issues Tracker and we will see if a patch can be made!
